source_section: Strengthen Canadian democracy, safety, and privacy in the AI era
action_summary: Canada will modernize consumer privacy legislation to enshrine a fundamental right to privacy, safeguard children’s information from exploitation and harm, and strengthen people’s control over their personal data.
mapped_personas: Concerned Canadian; Future Canadian; Small Business Owner
mapping_note: Directly answers trust, data rights, and customer-data accountability questions.

source_section: Strengthen Canadian democracy, safety, and privacy in the AI era
action_summary: Canada will introduce online safety laws to protect Canadians in the digital age, ensuring citizens, children, and customers are safeguarded.
mapped_personas: Concerned Canadian; Future Canadian; Small Business Owner
mapping_note: Maps to deepfake, child safety, and customer trust concerns.

source_section: Strengthen Canadian democracy, safety, and privacy in the AI era
action_summary: Canada will protect elections and democratic institutions from AI‑enabled misinformation and foreign interference.
mapped_personas: Concerned Canadian; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Primarily a public trust and democracy action.

source_section: Strengthen Canadian democracy, safety, and privacy in the AI era
action_summary: For the Government’s own use of personal information, the Government will continue its review of the Privacy Act to meets the needs of Canadians in the digital age. This will include considerations around transparency, privacy, and alignment with international standards.
mapped_personas: Concerned Canadian; Workplace AI Adopter; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Relevant to citizens and public servants using AI in government services.

source_section: Ensure Canadian AI infrastructure is safe and trustworthy
action_summary: Canada will invest $50 million to expand the capabilities of the Canadian AI Safety Institute to track emerging AI risks, advance technical research, and to conduct transparent evaluations of AI models.
mapped_personas: Concerned Canadian; AI Entrepreneur; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Supports trust, safety evaluation, and credible Canadian AI products.

source_section: Ensure Canadian AI infrastructure is safe and trustworthy
action_summary: Canada will work on AI transparency, including capabilities like watermarking of AI-generated content, so Canadians can better understand when they are interacting with AI systems and AI-generated content.
mapped_personas: Concerned Canadian; Small Business Owner; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Directly maps to labels, synthetic media, and trustworthy customer disclosure.

source_section: Ensure Canadian AI infrastructure is safe and trustworthy
action_summary: Canada will proactively work with frontier AI companies and international partners, leveraging our strengths in AI safety and cybersecurity, to ensure that Canadians and critical systems are protected from cyber and national security threats from advanced AI systems.
mapped_personas: Concerned Canadian; Future Canadian; Other
mapping_note: Important but mostly national-security and advanced-system governance rather than one persona's everyday workflow.

source_section: Ensure Canadian AI infrastructure is safe and trustworthy
action_summary: Canada will create a Canada Trusted AI Certification program to help Canadians identify trustworthy AI products in the marketplace.
mapped_personas: Small Business Owner; Concerned Canadian; AI Entrepreneur; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Helps buyers, citizens, and vendors interpret trust signals.

source_section: Ensure Canadian AI infrastructure is safe and trustworthy
action_summary: Canada will renew funding for the Standards Council of Canada’s AI Program to support our standardization ecosystem, shape global AI standards, and grow a robust AI quality assurance ecosystem. This work will enable standards-based AI testing, certification, interoperability, and global market access.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Small Business Owner; Workplace AI Adopter; Other
mapping_note: Most useful as market and implementation infrastructure; also informs workplace governance.

source_section: Ensure Canadian AI infrastructure is safe and trustworthy
action_summary: Canada will accelerate, in partnership with law enforcement and security and intelligence agencies, applied AI research, testing, and deployment of Canadian technologies for fraud and extortion prevention, cyber defence, threat detection, and data protection.
mapped_personas: Concerned Canadian; Future Canadian; Other
mapping_note: Citizen safety is the clearest fit, with a strong security-agency component.

source_section: Foundational literacy to build an AI-skilled nation
action_summary: Canada will create a National AI Literacy Initiative that will offer entry-level AI training accessible to all Canadians.
mapped_personas: Student / Future Worker; Workplace AI Adopter; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Broad literacy action, strongest fit with students and future workers.

source_section: Foundational literacy to build an AI-skilled nation
action_summary: AI literacy content will reach 1 million entry level post-secondary students and train more than 3,000 educators with AI learning kits in their classrooms. Free, accessible AI learning will include practical courses and sector-relevant modules.
mapped_personas: Student / Future Worker; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Direct education pipeline action.

source_section: Foundational literacy to build an AI-skilled nation
action_summary: Canada will ensure all post-secondary students have access to trusted AI agents, putting capable, personal AI tools in the hands of the next generation of Canadian workers, researchers, and innovators.
mapped_personas: Student / Future Worker
mapping_note: Directly maps to student tools and readiness.

source_section: Foundational literacy to build an AI-skilled nation
action_summary: Through the National AI Literacy initiative, Canada will empower public libraries and community organizations, long trusted as hubs for learning, as natural partners to bring AI literacy initiatives into every community, especially those in rural, remote, and northern regions.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Student / Future Worker; Other
mapping_note: Community-access action that goes beyond a single user role.

source_section: Foundational literacy to build an AI-skilled nation
action_summary: Canada is investing $50 million over five years through Budget 2025 to modernize the Job Bank with AI-powered job matching that automatically aligns people with opportunities matching their skills profiles and has launched a national online training platform connecting adults to free and low-cost short-duration courses, including sector-specific AI literacy skills.
mapped_personas: Workplace AI Adopter; Student / Future Worker; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Maps to career transition, job matching, and practical training access.

source_section: Foundational literacy to build an AI-skilled nation
action_summary: Canada will invest $30 million in CanCode to fund not-for-profit organizations to deliver free digital skills training — including coding, AI, and emerging technologies — to youth from kindergarten to grade 12 and their educators, with emphasis on reaching underrepresented groups.
mapped_personas: Student / Future Worker; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Youth pipeline action for future workers.

source_section: Applied workforce skills driving AI adoption
action_summary: Canada will create up to 90,000 AI-related job opportunities, including 45,000 through the Student Work Placement Program and Canada Summer Jobs, and 35,000 through other initiatives such as the Skills for Success Program, and 10,000 through the Mitacs ADOPT and AI+X programs.
mapped_personas: Student / Future Worker; Future Canadian; Workplace AI Adopter
mapping_note: Direct work-placement and early-career pathway action.

source_section: Applied workforce skills driving AI adoption
action_summary: Canada will assess training and upskill offerings for mid-career workers, including in skilled trades, to scale-up employer-led training nationwide with a strong priority on AI-related skills. This will also include work with Colleges, CÉGEPs and Polytechnics as applied AI leaders to leverage regional reach, applied research capacity, and SME partnerships to deliver hands-on adoption and improve workforce productivity.
mapped_personas: Workplace AI Adopter; Student / Future Worker; Small Business Owner
mapping_note: Worker transition and employer-led training action.

source_section: Applied workforce skills driving AI adoption
action_summary: Canada will accelerate AI adoption across female-dominated sectors most exposed to early disruption through the existing Women’s Program — unlocking new economic opportunities, softening transition shocks, and confronting AI-fueled online abuse that disproportionately targets women.
mapped_personas: Workplace AI Adopter; Future Canadian; Other
mapping_note: Important equity and labour-transition action with a sector/gender lens.

source_section: Applied workforce skills driving AI adoption
action_summary: Canada will track and assess the societal, labour market, and economic impacts of AI to guide policy and maximize benefits, leveraging Statistics Canada’s Artificial Intelligence and Technology Measurement Program.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Workplace AI Adopter; Concerned Canadian
mapping_note: Accountability and measurement action for public outcomes.

source_section: Supporting Indigenous leadership in AI
action_summary: Canada will support and amplify Indigenous-led AI initiatives that reinforce cultural expression and linguistic vitality in Canada and around the world, building on existing efforts such as the Indigenous Languages Program at Canadian Heritage, the Indigenous Languages Technology Program at the National Research Council, and First Languages AI Reality at Mila.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Concerned Canadian; Other
mapping_note: Cross-cutting Indigenous governance and cultural action; not reducible to one app persona.

source_section: Ensure AI reflects Canadian identity, culture, and inclusion
action_summary: Canada will work on the adoption of tools that protect and promote the French language, including in Québec and in Canada’s vibrant Francophone minority communities. Critically, AI systems deployed within the government will perform equally well in both official languages.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Workplace AI Adopter; Other
mapping_note: Public-service, language, and inclusion action.

source_section: Ensure AI reflects Canadian identity, culture, and inclusion
action_summary: Canada will establish a $50 million Creative Technology Program to support Canadian creators in using AI on their own terms.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Other
mapping_note: Creator-focused action; maps to Other because the creator persona was removed.

source_section: Ensure AI reflects Canadian identity, culture, and inclusion
action_summary: Canada will promote the world’s first AI equity-based national standard on accessible AI to drive inclusive and accessible AI and remove accessibility barriers from AI systems, and ensure Canadian AI reflects the Accessible Canada Act principles.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Concerned Canadian; Other
mapping_note: Inclusion, accessibility, and public benefit action.

source_section: Ensure AI reflects Canadian identity, culture, and inclusion
action_summary: The Government of Canada commits to applying Gender-Based Analysis Plus in a meaningful way across policy design, skills development, innovation, and governance to ensure that AI reflects our values, protects those most impacted, and leads to outcomes that are safe, inclusive, and beneficial for all Canadians.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Concerned Canadian; Workplace AI Adopter; Other
mapping_note: Cross-cutting equity lens for policy and implementation.

source_section: Accelerating AI adoption across Canadian SMEs
action_summary: Canada will utilize the LIFT program, a $500 million initiative from the Business Development Bank of Canada (BDC) to help Canadian SMEs access financing to incorporate AI tools in their operations.
mapped_personas: Small Business Owner
mapping_note: Direct SME financing action.

source_section: Accelerating AI adoption across Canadian SMEs
action_summary: Canada will invest $500 million to expand and enhance the Regional Artificial Intelligence Initiative delivered through Regional Development Agencies to accelerate adoption and commercialization of AI across the country.
mapped_personas: Small Business Owner; AI Entrepreneur; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Regional adoption and commercialization support.

source_section: Accelerating AI adoption across Canadian SMEs
action_summary: Canada will support the development of an AI Literacy and Adoption Assessment tool and other online resources that help SMEs and entrepreneurs assess their AI readiness, identify practical use cases, understand potential business impacts in a low-risk environment, and connect with critical programs and development agencies.
mapped_personas: Small Business Owner; Workplace AI Adopter
mapping_note: Practical adoption guidance for owners and teams.

source_section: Accelerating AI adoption across Canadian SMEs
action_summary: Canada will provide targeted support to strengthen AI adoption among Canadian entrepreneurs through the Small Business and Entrepreneurship Development Program.
mapped_personas: Small Business Owner; AI Entrepreneur
mapping_note: Entrepreneurship and adoption support.

source_section: Accelerating AI adoption across Canadian SMEs
action_summary: Canada will leverage the SR&ED tax credit and the Productivity Super-Deduction announced in Budget 2025 to catalyze private sector investment and make innovation more affordable.
mapped_personas: Small Business Owner; AI Entrepreneur
mapping_note: Tax and investment incentive action for business adoption and innovation.

source_section: Solving national challenges through AI missions
action_summary: Canada will launch a new AI Missions Program to advance targeted, high-impact projects that deliver significant public good and demonstrate meaningful improvements in Canadians' lives. The first mission will commit $200 million to improving health outcomes for Canadians. More missions will follow in other priority sectors.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Concerned Canadian; AI Entrepreneur
mapping_note: Public-good mission action with health as the first visible benefit area.

source_section: Solving national challenges through AI missions
action_summary: Canada will launch sector-specific Workforce Alliances across six priority sectors to bring together governments, employers, unions, post-secondary institutions, and Indigenous partners to identify skills gaps, coordinate talent pipelines, and align public-private investment with workforce transformation needs across essential economic sectors.
mapped_personas: Workplace AI Adopter; Student / Future Worker; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Workforce coordination action across sectors.

source_section: Transforming Public Service Delivery with AI
action_summary: Canada will accelerate the procurement and delivery of AI solutions across the federal government through the Office of Digital Transformation.
mapped_personas: Workplace AI Adopter; AI Entrepreneur; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Public-service implementation and government buyer action.

source_section: Transforming Public Service Delivery with AI
action_summary: Canada will launch the Prime Minister’s Innovation Fellows Program to recruit and deploy technical talent that rapidly builds the internal muscle, operational capacity, and commercial fluency required to procure, evaluate, and deploy AI systems effectively within government.
mapped_personas: Workplace AI Adopter; AI Entrepreneur; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Public-sector talent and delivery-capacity action.

source_section: Sovereign compute, cloud, and connectivity
action_summary: Canada will build a world-leading public supercomputer, giving Canadian researchers and SMEs access to secure, sovereign, high-performance compute for cutting-edge public and industry-driven innovation.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Small Business Owner; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Compute access is a core founder and commercialization need.

source_section: Sovereign compute, cloud, and connectivity
action_summary: Canada will leverage government and industrial AI workloads and crowd-in private capital to significantly expand sovereign compute and cloud infrastructure. This will support the construction of large‑scale AI data centres that can scale to at least 100 megawatts (MW), and that are designed to serve a broad spectrum of Canadian clients. These partnerships are being finalized, and have proposed providing 850 MW of compute capacity by 2030, with scaling capacity of up to 2.3 GW with corresponding investments in the tens of billions.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; AI Entrepreneur; Small Business Owner; Concerned Canadian
mapping_note: Infrastructure action with public-benefit, sovereignty, and business-access implications.

source_section: Sovereign compute, cloud, and connectivity
action_summary: Canada will expand diverse high-capacity fibre lines and satellite connectivity, resulting in resilient network infrastructure with sovereign capabilities.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Small Business Owner; Other
mapping_note: Connectivity infrastructure action that mostly sits below the persona layer.

source_section: Sovereign compute, cloud, and connectivity
action_summary: Canada will further enhance and secure its chip design and fabrication capabilities, building on the recent announcement to spin off the National Research Council’s Photonics Fabrication Centre.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Other
mapping_note: Hardware supply-chain action with limited direct persona fit.

source_section: Sovereign compute, cloud, and connectivity
action_summary: Canada will invest to build and reinforce secure digital systems used for Government operations, including sovereign cloud, AI, cyber and quantum initiatives.
mapped_personas: Concerned Canadian; Workplace AI Adopter; Future Canadian; Other
mapping_note: Trust, public-service, and national infrastructure action.

source_section: Unlocking the power of data
action_summary: Canada will invest $100 million to launch the Health Sector Data Space, in partnership with the Canadian Institute for Health Information (CIHI), to link secure, private and standardized datasets to strengthen clinical trials, health services research and performance measurements.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Concerned Canadian; AI Entrepreneur
mapping_note: Health-data action balancing care improvement, privacy, and innovation.

source_section: Unlocking the power of data
action_summary: Canada will also invest $100 million to expand VITAL in five additional provinces, supporting its ability to leverage clinical data from hospitals and enable health data innovation that reduces mortality rates and accelerates critical care for Canadians.
mapped_personas: Future Canadian; Concerned Canadian; AI Entrepreneur
mapping_note: Health-system and health-data innovation action.

source_section: Anchoring world-class research talent in Canada
action_summary: Canada will strengthen our network of national AI institutes and increase the Canada CIFAR AI Chairs program from 130 to nearly 200 researchers. This means more world-class AI talent working in Canada, more ideas moving from research labs into businesses and public services, and more support to help Canadians understand and use AI.
mapped_personas: Student / Future Worker; AI Entrepreneur; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Talent pipeline and research-commercialization action.

source_section: Anchoring world-class research talent in Canada
action_summary: Canada will expand the Global Talent Stream to accelerate entry and onboarding of highly-skilled AI workers, and align measures for permanent residency to retain the talent Canada recruits.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Student / Future Worker; Other
mapping_note: Talent attraction matters most to scaling companies and research capacity.

source_section: Creating an investment flywheel that rewards responsible risk-taking
action_summary: Canada will establish a $500 million Canadian Tech Growth Fund to help close the scale-up capital gap facing Canada’s most promising AI companies. The Fund will provide flexible growth capital and investment support, and enable the federal government, at times, to take equity stakes in the most promising Canadian AI firms. This will help them attract private capital, compete globally, retain talent and intellectual property, and remain anchored in Canada. Where appropriate, Canada will leverage its recently announced Sovereign Wealth Fund to further support emerging national champions.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Core scale-up capital action with public-upside implications.

source_section: Creating an investment flywheel that rewards responsible risk-taking
action_summary: By Budget 2026, the Department of Finance will work with experts to explore mechanisms that encourage Canadians to reinvest gains earned from successful tech companies into new Canadian AI startups.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Other
mapping_note: Startup-capital recycling action with narrow founder/investor fit.

source_section: Creating an investment flywheel that rewards responsible risk-taking
action_summary: Canada will also leverage $1.75 billion of federal investments and commitments announced in Budget 2025 to stimulate private sector investment in venture capital and address access to capital gaps for innovative companies, including those in AI.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur
mapping_note: Scale-up financing action.

source_section: Creating an investment flywheel that rewards responsible risk-taking
action_summary: Canada will establish the federal government as a strategic anchor customer and leverage the Buy Canadian policy to provide domestic scale-ups with the revenue and validation they need to successfully export their solutions globally.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Workplace AI Adopter; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Government customer action for founders and public-service adoption.

source_section: Accelerating SME commercialization on Canadian infrastructure
action_summary: Canada will provide Canadian SMEs with an additional $700 million in affordable sovereign compute through an expansion of the Compute Access Fund.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Small Business Owner
mapping_note: Compute access for commercialization and adoption.

source_section: Accelerating SME commercialization on Canadian infrastructure
action_summary: Canada will invest $130 million for commercialization programs across the National AI Institutes, including for Founders-in-Residence, to cultivate a new generation of AI entrepreneurs.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Student / Future Worker
mapping_note: Founder creation and research-to-market action.

source_section: Accelerating SME commercialization on Canadian infrastructure
action_summary: Canada will assess the full continuum of innovation programs to better align existing innovation instruments so that the path from research to market is shorter, clearer, and built around the needs of Canadian entrepreneurs.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur
mapping_note: Founder and commercialization-system action.

source_section: Accelerating SME commercialization on Canadian infrastructure
action_summary: Canada will leverage $159 million invested in Budget 2025 through the Elevate IP and IP Assist programs to protect Canadian intellectual property and support SMEs to commercialize their intangible assets in the global marketplace.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Small Business Owner
mapping_note: IP and commercialization support for firms.

source_section: Championing our foundation models
action_summary: Canada will anchor its homegrown foundation model capabilities at home, and support their international growth by exporting them to a growing global market actively seeking trusted alternatives.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Future Canadian; Other
mapping_note: Foundation-model sovereignty and export action.

source_section: Championing our foundation models
action_summary: Canada will support research in Canadian foundation models built with safety at their core, drawing on the country's deep bench of AI safety research to make trustworthiness a defining feature of Canadian models.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Concerned Canadian; Future Canadian
mapping_note: Connects model development with trust and safety.

source_section: Spearheading a multinational sovereign technology alliance
action_summary: Canada will expand the newly formed Sovereign Technology Alliance to enable secure and interoperable AI capabilities and open procurement opportunities for domestic champions. This will accelerate responsible AI adoption, de-risk private-sector investment and cross-border deployment, reduce strategic dependence on dominant technology ecosystems while safeguarding rights and data, and expand joint research, talent, and investment opportunities.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Future Canadian; Other
mapping_note: Market access and strategic-autonomy action.

source_section: Spearheading a multinational sovereign technology alliance
action_summary: Canada will leverage the Trade Commissioner Service and diplomatic networks to attract foreign investment in AI, showcase Canadian champions abroad, and enable new markets for Canadian firms.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur
mapping_note: Export and investment support for Canadian AI firms.

source_section: Advancing open-source AI for resilience and choice
action_summary: Canada will lead a global, multi-stakeholder effort to invest in and sustain open-source AI development in the public interest, working with like-minded countries, Canadian AI institutes, researchers, civil society, industry, and global open-source organizations.
mapped_personas: Small Business Owner; AI Entrepreneur; Future Canadian; Other
mapping_note: Open-source ecosystem action with public-interest and adoption implications.

source_section: Advancing open-source AI for resilience and choice
action_summary: Canada will support the responsible adoption of open-source AI by Canadian researchers, SMEs, not-for-profit organizations, and public-interest innovators, working to create an inventory and shared library of access tools that are transparent, adaptable, secure, and aligned with Canadian needs.
mapped_personas: Small Business Owner; AI Entrepreneur; Workplace AI Adopter; Other
mapping_note: Practical tool-access action for SMEs, innovators, and teams.

source_section: Expanding international partnerships and AI collaboration
action_summary: Europe: Germany is Canada’s anchor partner through the Sovereign Technology Alliance, with broader cooperation on critical minerals, clean energy, aerospace and defence. The UK is advancing joint work on AI safety, standards and defence AI. France is coordinating with Canada on AI governance under its G7 Presidency. The EU-Canada Strategic Partnership of the Future will embed AI alongside critical minerals, clean energy and defence. Finland and Norway add complementary strengths in connectivity, space, digitalization and critical minerals.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Other
mapping_note: International partnership action; relevant to firms but mostly diplomatic/economic infrastructure.

source_section: Expanding international partnerships and AI collaboration
action_summary: Indo-Pacific: Canada, Australia, and India are formalizing a trilateral technology and innovation partnership covering AI, quantum, trade missions, and private-sector matchmaking. Canada is also collaborating with Australia's AI Safety Institute on shared evaluation expertise. Japan offers opportunities in semiconductors, robotics, industrial AI, and quantum computing through the modernized Canada-Japan Joint Economic Committee.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Other
mapping_note: International market and technology partnership action.

source_section: Expanding international partnerships and AI collaboration
action_summary: Middle East: The UAE is drawing sovereign wealth investment into Canadian data infrastructure and AI projects. Qatar is partnering with Canada on AI and emerging technologies, opening Gulf market access and supporting Vision 2030 diversification. Saudi Arabia is cooperating on tech and AI through the relaunched Joint Economic Commission.
mapped_personas: AI Entrepreneur; Other
mapping_note: International investment and market-access action with limited direct persona fit.
